title: Speedboat Hire in Puerto Banus: Licence and Costs 2026
html:
No-licence speedboat hire in Puerto Banus lets visitors take a small motorboat out of Marina Puerto Jose Banus from roughly EUR 90 to EUR 180 for a half-day, with a skipper or under a power cap. BoatHire24 lists verified boats here, so you can compare a day on the Costa del Sol before you commit.

Where to set off and which boat to choose

Most speedboat trips leave from Marina Puerto Jose Banus, the marble-quayed harbour at the heart of the resort. From there you have a sheltered run of coast in either direction. These departure points all sit along this stretch of Andalusia.

  • Marina Puerto Jose Banus: the main marina, with day berths and the largest concentration of charter boats.
  • Marbella town and its Old Town beaches: a short hop east along the shore.
  • Puerto Deportivo de Marbella: a smaller working marina between Banus and Marbella centre.
  • Cabopino: a quieter anchorage further east towards Mijas.
  • Estepona: a calmer harbour town to the west, reachable on a longer day.

Do you need a licence to rent a speedboat in Puerto Banus?

No, you can hire certain low-power boats in Spain without a licence, while larger speedboats are taken out with a skipper or with your own valid certificate. The principle in Spanish waters is that small craft below a defined engine and length threshold may be driven without a formal qualification, and the operator confirms the exact requirements for each boat at booking. Browsing options early, including a wider yacht charter in Puerto Banus No, helps you see which listings are skippered and which are self-drive.

The right boat depends on group size and how far you plan to cruise. Speedboats suit short, lively trips; larger motor yachts suit a full day.

  1. No-licence motorboat: best for a first outing close to the marina, seats up to 5 guests, from EUR 90 per half-day.
  2. Mid-size speedboat: good for coastal cruising and swim stops, seats up to 8, from EUR 250 per day. A 6-metre open boat such as the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP charter page shows the kind of agile day boat that handles this coast well.
  3. RIB: ideal for watersports and reaching coves quickly, seats up to 10, from EUR 300 per day.
  4. Sports cruiser: suited to longer days with a cabin and sunbathing space, seats up to 11, from EUR 600 per day.
  5. Motor yacht: for groups wanting comfort and crew, seats up to 12, from EUR 1,400 per day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

Prices vary with season, boat size and whether a skipper is included. The bands below give a realistic picture for the 2026 season.

  • A shared dolphin or coastal tour typically runs from EUR 25 to EUR 45 per person.
  • A small no-licence motorboat for a half-day starts at about EUR 90 in EUR.
  • A catamaran for a full day generally ranges from EUR 800 to EUR 1,600 in EUR.
  • A crewed motor yacht usually starts around EUR 1,400 per day and rises with size in EUR.

How does booking a speedboat actually work?

You pick dates, choose a boat, confirm whether it is self-drive or skippered, and the operator sends final details before you cast off. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own, and listings state capacity, fuel policy and meeting point. Marina Puerto Jose Banus sits roughly 3 nautical miles west of Marbella, a comfortable cruise that many half-day trips include.

When is the best season, and what about licences?

High season runs June to September with warm, settled seas, while licence-free options widen your choice of self-drive boats. The dominant local wind is the Levante, an easterly that can build chop along the Costa del Sol, with the milder Poniente blowing from the west. Mornings are usually calmer for first-timers and for swim stops.

Book a morning slot in July or August: the sea off Puerto Banus is typically flattest before midday, and the Levante wind tends to strengthen through the afternoon, making the return leg bumpier.

At a glance

  • Price band: from EUR 90 half-day to EUR 1,400 plus per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: 4 hours half-day, 8 hours full day
  • Capacity: 5 to 12 guests depending on boat
  • Currency: EUR
  • Main departure: Marina Puerto Jose Banus

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I drive a speedboat myself without a licence?

Yes, smaller low-power boats can be self-driven without a formal qualification in Spanish waters, subject to engine and length limits. The operator confirms the exact threshold for each listing, so always check first.

How many people fit on a speedboat here?

Most speedboats on this coast carry between 5 and 12 guests depending on length and design. Listings state the maximum capacity clearly, so groups can match the boat to their party size before booking.

Is fuel included in the rental price?

Fuel is usually charged separately from the base rental, and a refundable deposit is common. Each listing sets out the fuel policy and deposit, so you can budget the full cost in advance.

How far in advance should I book?

Booking a week or more ahead is wise for July and August, when popular boats sell out quickly. Outside peak season you can often secure a speedboat with a day or two of notice.

A licence-free speedboat is one of the easiest ways to enjoy the Costa del Sol, and comparing verified listings on the BoatHire24 marketplace makes it simple to match a boat to your group and budget for the 2026 season. Confirm the licence and fuel details with your operator, then set off from Puerto Banus.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
